title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: How to Make Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted — Easy Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted. This easy Middle Eastern recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

description: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted brings the rich flavors of the Middle East to your table with ease. This delightful dish combines tender, spiced lamb with aromatic rice, peas, and toasted nuts, creating a truly festive and satisfying meal. It's a celebration of traditional spices and textures, perfect for a family gathering or special occasion.

ingredients.item: Cooked lamb shoulder or leg, shredded

instructions.title: Prepare the Rice
instructions.text: Rinse the basmati rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Soak it in warm water for at least 20 minutes, then drain it very well. This step helps ensure fluffy, separate grains of rice.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the vegetable oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until it softens and becomes translucent, which usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Be careful not to brown it too much.

instructions.title: Build the Rice Base
instructions.text: Add the drained basmati rice, ouzi spice blend (a mix of cinnamon, allspice, cardamom, and nutmeg works wonderfully), salt, and black pepper to the pot with the sautéed onions. Stir gently for about 2 minutes to lightly toast the spices and coat the rice grains evenly.

instructions.title: Cook the Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: Pour in the chicken or lamb broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot tightly and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. During the last 5 minutes of cooking, stir in the frozen green peas.

instructions.title: Warm Lamb and Toast Nuts
instructions.text: While the rice is cooking, gently warm the pre-cooked shredded lamb in a separate pan over low heat. Add a splash of broth or water if needed to keep it moist. In a dry pan, toast the mixed nuts (almonds and pine nuts are traditional) over medium heat until they are fragrant and lightly golden, about 2-3 minutes. Watch them carefully to prevent burning.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Once the rice is cooked, fluff it gently with a fork. Transfer the aromatic ouzi rice to a large serving platter. Arrange the warm shredded lamb over the rice. Finally, sprinkle generously with the toasted mixed nuts and fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately as a complete and satisfying meal.

tips: For an authentic touch, using homemade lamb broth for cooking the rice significantly enhances the overall depth of flavor in the dish.
If you prefer a richer taste, brown the shredded lamb lightly in a little ghee before adding it to the assembled dish. This adds a lovely caramelized note.
Serve Lamb Ouzi with a side of plain yogurt or a simple fresh salad like fattoush or a cucumber and tomato salad to provide a refreshing contrast to the rich flavors.
article:
Unveiling a truly majestic centerpiece for any special occasion, Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted stands as a testament to the rich culinary heritage of the Middle East. This dish is more than just a meal; it's an experience, a celebration of flavor, tradition, and shared joy. Imagine a succulent, fall-off-the-bone lamb, infused with aromatic spices, nestled atop a bed of fragrant rice studded with nuts and often dried fruits. The slow roasting process transforms humble ingredients into a symphony of textures and tastes, making it an unforgettable dish that commands attention and admiration. If you're looking to impress guests or simply indulge in an authentic Middle Eastern feast, mastering the art of Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a journey well worth taking.

## What is Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted?

Lamb Ouzi (also spelled "Qoozi" or "Ghozi") is a classic Middle Eastern dish renowned for its grandeur and deep, complex flavors. At its heart lies a whole or large cut of lamb, traditionally slow-roasted until incredibly tender. The meat is then served over a generous mound of spiced Basmati rice, often mixed with minced lamb or beef, toasted nuts like pine nuts and almonds, and sometimes sweet elements like raisins or apricots. While variations exist across different regions – some might serve it with a thin bread (markouk) or a rich sauce – the essence remains the same: a celebratory dish that showcases the melt-in-your-mouth tenderness of lamb achieved through patient, slow cooking. It’s a dish often reserved for significant gatherings, holidays, and weddings, embodying the generosity and hospitality of Middle Eastern culture. The commitment to slow roasting an entire lamb, or a substantial cut, is what truly elevates Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted into a culinary masterpiece.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on your Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted adventure, gather these essential ingredients:

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb shoulder or leg (5-7 lbs / 2.5-3.5 kg), bone-in
*   1 cup plain full-fat yogurt
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   8-10 cloves garlic, minced
*   2 tablespoons ground cumin
*   2 tablespoons ground coriander
*   1 tablespoon sweet paprika
*   1 tablespoon dried oregano
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1 teaspoon ground allspice
*   1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
*   Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
*   2 large onions, quartered
*   4 carrots, roughly chopped
*   4 celery stalks, roughly chopped
*   4 cups lamb or chicken broth

For the Spiced Rice:
*   3 cups Basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   1/4 cup olive oil or ghee
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   500g ground lamb or beef (optional, for richer rice)
*   2 teaspoons ground cumin
*   2 teaspoons ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/2 teaspoon ground allspice
*   Salt and pepper to taste
*   5 cups lamb or chicken broth
*   1/2 cup pine nuts, toasted
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, toasted
*   1/2 cup raisins or dried cranberries (optional)
*   Fresh parsley and mint, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the surface of the lamb in a diamond pattern, about 1/2 inch deep. In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, olive oil, minced garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, oregano, cinnamon, allspice, nutmeg, salt, and pepper to create the marinade. Generously rub the marinade all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scored areas. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan, cover, and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate.

2.  **Roast the Lamb:** Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Arrange the quartered onions, carrots, and celery at the bottom of the roasting pan around the lamb. Pour the 4 cups of broth into the pan. Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil or a lid. Roast for 4-5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and an internal temperature of 200°F (93°C) is reached. For the last 30-45 minutes, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown and crisp up slightly. Once done, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a large cutting board, tent with foil, and let it rest for at least 30 minutes before shredding or carving. Strain the pan juices and defat them; these juices will be used for the rice or as a sauce.

3.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice:** While the lamb rests, prepare the rice. Heat olive oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. If using ground meat, add it now and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat. Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cardamom, allspice, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the rinsed Basmati rice and stir to coat evenly with the spices. Pour in the 5 cups of broth (you can substitute some with the strained lamb pan juices for extra flavor). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Turn off the heat and let it steam, covered, for another 10 minutes. Fluff with a fork.

4.  **Assemble and Serve:** In a large serving platter, spread the spiced rice evenly. Gently shred the rested Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted and arrange it generously over the rice. Sprinkle with toasted pine nuts, almonds, and optional raisins. Garnish with plenty of fresh chopped parsley and mint. Serve immediately, with the defatted pan juices on the side.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted requires a little patience and attention to detail.
*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on the marination time. The longer the lamb sits in the yogurt-spice mixture, the more tender and flavorful it will become. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Low and Slow:** The secret to truly tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb is low and slow roasting. Resist the urge to crank up the heat; patience will be rewarded with incredibly succulent meat.
*   **Rest the Meat:** After roasting, always allow the lamb to rest, tented with foil, for at least 30 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and moist final product.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Toasting pine nuts and almonds beforehand significantly enhances their flavor and aroma, adding a crucial textural and taste element to the Ouzi.
*   **Don't Waste the Juices:** The strained pan juices from the lamb are liquid gold. Use them to cook your rice for an extra layer of flavor, or serve them as a rich, savory sauce alongside the dish.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Start with a good quality cut of lamb and fresh spices for the most impactful flavor profile.

## Serving Suggestions

Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a grand dish meant to be the star of your table. It’s typically served as a magnificent centerpiece, with the tender lamb majestically presented over the fragrant rice. To complement its richness and provide a refreshing contrast, consider serving it with:
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like tzatziki) or a dollop of labneh (strained yogurt) provides a cooling counterpoint.
*   **Fresh Salads:** Tabbouleh (parsley and bulgur salad), Fattoush (bread salad), or a simple green salad with a lemon-tahini dressing offer freshness and acidity.
*   **Warm Pita Bread:** Perfect for scooping up any extra rice or sauce.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) add a tangy crunch that cuts through the richness.
*   **Roasted Vegetables:** Alongside the lamb, you could roast additional vegetables like bell peppers or zucchini for more variety.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or what you have on hand:
*   **Different Cuts of Lamb:** If a whole shoulder or leg is too much, you can use smaller cuts like lamb shanks or a boneless leg, adjusting cooking times accordingly. The essence of the slow roast remains.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Feel free to adjust the spice blend to your liking. Add a pinch of cayenne for a subtle kick, or more cardamom for a sweeter, more aromatic profile.
*   **Rice Fillings:** Instead of or in addition to ground meat, you can add cooked chickpeas, lentils, or other vegetables to the rice for added texture and nutrients.
*   **Nut-Free Option:** If there are nut allergies, simply omit the pine nuts and almonds. The dish will still be incredibly flavorful.
*   **Dried Fruits:** Experiment with different dried fruits in the rice, such as dried apricots, dates, or even figs, for varying notes of sweetness.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi:** While not traditional lamb ouzi, you can create a vegetarian version by using a base of richly spiced rice with roasted vegetables, lentils, and chickpeas, then topping it with toasted nuts and fresh herbs.

Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is more than just a recipe; it’s an invitation to explore the heart of Middle Eastern hospitality and culinary tradition. The effort put into its preparation is generously rewarded with an extraordinary meal that will leave a lasting impression on anyone fortunate enough to gather around your table. So, embrace the slow cooking, savor the aromas, and prepare to create a truly unforgettable dining experience with this magnificent dish.
